'''Monty Betham''' (born 27 September 1952 in [[Savai'i]]) was a [[Samoa]]n professional boxer who fought boxers such as [[Tony Mundine (boxer)|Tony Mundine]], and  [[Lance Revill]] in a professional career beginning in 1973 until 1984.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.boxrec.com/list_bouts.php?human_id=26777&cat=boxer |title=Monty Betham |publisher=BoxRec |access-date=2009-11-15 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121010192056/http://boxrec.com/list_bouts.php?human_id=26777&cat=boxer |archive-date=2012-10-10 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He is the father of former [[New Zealand Warriors]] rugby league star [[Monty Betham]]. He is also the older brother of [[Richard Betham]], who won a bronze medal for Western Samoa in boxing at the [[1978 Commonwealth Games]].<ref>{{cite news | title=McElwaine out for gold | newspaper=Sydney Morning Herald | date=11 August 1978 | page=27}}</ref>

In July 1975 he won the [[List of Commonwealth Boxing Council champions|Commonwealth middleweight boxing title]], after defeating Carlos Marks.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/110649305 |title=Likely challengers |work= The Canberra Times |page=36 |date=26 July 1975 |access-date=23 August 2021 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref> he retained the title in February 1976 after defeating Semi Bula.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/250323258 |title=Betham wins with a TKO |work= Papua New Guinea Post-Courier |page=23 |date=25 February 1976 |access-date=23 August 2021 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref> He lost the title to [[Al Korovou]] in March 1978.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/250341283 |title=Gillon wants more fights |work=Papua New Guinea Post-Courier |page=36 |date=5 April 1978 |access-date=23 August 2021 |via=National Library of Australia}}</ref>
